_comment: LEAN storyboard — each scene holds only its scene-specific image_prompt (subject), optional accent target, and (animated) animation_prompt (motion). Style-card prefix, scene_type recipe and accent_hex are applied at generation time by generate_assets.py — no boilerplate stored per scene.

scenes.image_prompt: a lone village woman as a tiny silhouette at the rim of the vast square stepwell at dawn, the great inverted staircase falling away beneath her, first golden light on parchment-toned sandstone
scenes.animation_prompt: very slow push-in toward the woman at the rim; soft dawn haze; faint parallax on the steps below
scenes.narration_segment: Every morning, before the sun clears the rooftops of Abhaneri, a woman walks to the edge of a great square pit cut into the earth — and she begins to count.

scenes.image_prompt: close isometric of the double-flight steps folding back on themselves, a single tiny silhouette a few steps down
scenes.animation_prompt: slow downward drift following the descent; shadows shift gently
scenes.narration_segment: Down she goes: one step, then another, into a staircase that folds back on itself like a puzzle. And she counts, because the number matters.

scenes.image_prompt: clean sectional diagram of the stepwell with three faint horizontal waterlines marked high, mid and low, small step-count brackets beside each
scenes.narration_segment: Yesterday it was thirty steps before her clay pot touched water. In the flush weeks after the monsoon, it was barely ten. By the cruelest stretch of summer, it will be nearly a hundred and thirty.

scenes.image_prompt: cutaway section of the stepwell, the water surface at mid-height, clearly a movable level meeting the steps
scenes.animation_prompt: the water level glides up and down to 'meet' successive steps; subtle surface shimmer
scenes.narration_segment: She is not climbing down to the water. The water is rising and falling to meet her —

scenes.image_prompt: wide establishing view of the entire inverted-pyramid staircase from the rim, the full lattice of steps in golden light
scenes.animation_prompt: slow sweeping parallax across the vast step lattice
scenes.narration_segment: — and the staircase, three and a half thousand steps of it, was built, stone by stone, around that one stubborn fact.

scenes.image_prompt: a single carved step edge sharp in the foreground, the deep well softly falling away behind, an open question implied

texts.text: Why a staircase — not a well?

scenes.narration_segment: The question was never how they dug this deep. It's why they built a staircase instead of a well.

scenes.image_prompt: hero-wide of Chand Baori in golden light with generous clean sky negative space across the top for the wordmark overlay
scenes.animation_prompt: gentle slow push-in; dust motes catch the light
scenes.narration_segment: (Title sting — 2–3s wordmark + topic, music motif only, no narration.)

scenes.image_prompt: top-down cartographic schematic of arid north-west India / Rajasthan in tan tones, a small marker at Abhaneri near Jaipur, thin technical linework
scenes.narration_segment: Here is the constraint the builders of Abhaneri were handed, sometime in the eighth or ninth century. This is Rajasthan — one of the most arid corners of the Indian subcontinent.

scenes.image_prompt: wide arid Rajasthan landscape, a cracked dry riverbed, distant monsoon clouds massing on one horizon
scenes.animation_prompt: monsoon clouds sweep in then pass; ground shifts damp-to-parched, time-lapse feel
scenes.narration_segment: For a few weeks a year the monsoon arrives and the ground drinks its fill. Then, for the long remainder, almost nothing. The rivers thin to ribbons of sand.

scenes.image_prompt: earth cutaway showing the water-table as a distinct boundary between dry upper strata and saturated lower strata
scenes.animation_prompt: the water-table boundary sinks slowly downward through the strata
scenes.narration_segment: And underground, the water table — the depth at which you finally strike water — sinks, slowly and relentlessly, through the dry months.

scenes.image_prompt: simple vertical well in section — a narrow shaft to a fixed water level, rope and bucket
scenes.narration_segment: A well is a simple thing: dig a hole to where the water is, drop a rope, done.

scenes.image_prompt: the same vertical well, water beginning to drop below the fixed shaft bottom
scenes.animation_prompt: water level slips downward, leaving the bucket over dry air
scenes.narration_segment: But a well makes one assumption it has no right to make — that the water will stay put. In Abhaneri, it doesn't.

scenes.image_prompt: a well dug to the high-water mark, now a dry empty shaft in cracked summer strata
scenes.accent: a small dry-hole marker at the shaft base
scenes.animation_prompt: water retreats far below the shaft; faint heat shimmer
scenes.narration_segment: Dig your well down to the high-water mark and, by peak summer, the water has dropped metres below your reach: a dry hole.

scenes.image_prompt: a well dug to the summer low, flooded and overtopped after the monsoon, its draw point submerged
scenes.animation_prompt: water rises and floods over the well mouth
scenes.narration_segment: Dig instead to the summer low, and the monsoon floods the well and drowns your draw point under water you can't stand on.

scenes.image_prompt: a minimalist diagram panel — a single fixed point versus a tall vertical range bracket

texts.text: A fixed depth loses to a moving target

scenes.narration_segment: A single fixed depth always loses to a moving one.

scenes.image_prompt: wide of Chand Baori sitting quiet in its landscape, the real problem framed
scenes.narration_segment: That is the real problem at Chand Baori. Not depth. Not even thirst. It's that the thing they needed to reach would not hold still.

scenes.image_prompt: concept cutaway — replacing one fixed platform with a continuous stair spanning a full vertical range beside the water column
scenes.accent: the vertical range the stair covers
scenes.animation_prompt: a highlight sweeps down the whole range, showing every level is covered

texts.text: Build for a range, not a point

scenes.narration_segment: So they stopped trying to reach a point, and built for a range.

scenes.image_prompt: clean section of the inverted pyramid, square mouth narrowing to the tank
scenes.animation_prompt: the section reveals top-down; raking light crosses it
scenes.narration_segment: Picture the shape: an enormous inverted pyramid cut into the ground — square at the mouth, narrowing as it drops.

scenes.image_prompt: measured section with dimension markers: ~35 m across the mouth and ~20 m / ~13 storeys deep, a tiny human silhouette for scale
scenes.narration_segment: Roughly thirty-five metres to a side, and about twenty metres deep; call it thirteen storeys, straight down.

scenes.image_prompt: three walls of double-flight steps meeting at the corners in the tessellated diamond lattice, isometric

texts.text: Three sides · double flights of steps

scenes.narration_segment: Three of its four walls aren't walls at all. They're staircases — double flights of steps zig-zagging down in a tight lattice, landing after landing after landing.

scenes.image_prompt: section with a single step highlighted exactly at the waterline
scenes.accent: the active step at the waterline
scenes.animation_prompt: as the water level shifts slightly, the highlighted 'active' step tracks to remain at the surface

texts.text: Always a step at the waterline

scenes.narration_segment: And here is the elegant part. Because the steps cover the entire descent, there is always — always — a step sitting right at the waterline.

scenes.image_prompt: section with water HIGH near the top after monsoon, a short descent, tiny silhouette near the rim
scenes.animation_prompt: water sits high; the silhouette barely steps down to reach it
scenes.narration_segment: In the flush weeks after the rains, the water is high and you barely climb down at all.

scenes.image_prompt: section with water LOW near the base in high summer, a long descent, tiny silhouette walking far down
scenes.animation_prompt: the silhouette descends deep toward a low water level; dust in low light
scenes.narration_segment: In the heart of summer, the water has retreated deep into the earth, and you walk down, and down, to meet it.

scenes.image_prompt: section showing the building perfectly fixed while only the water surface moves up and down
scenes.animation_prompt: structure dead still; only the water level glides up and down
scenes.narration_segment: The building never moves. The water moves. And the staircase quietly guarantees that wherever the water is, a dry footing is waiting for you exactly there.

scenes.image_prompt: extreme close isometric of dry-laid sandstone blocks with open mortarless joints, precise fit
scenes.narration_segment: There's a second cleverness in the stonework, and you'd miss it if you weren't looking. The blocks are laid without mortar — nothing binding them.

scenes.image_prompt: cutaway of the mortarless wall with fine groundwater threading through the joints into the tank from all sides
scenes.animation_prompt: thin water threads trickle inward through the joints; the tank slowly fills
scenes.narration_segment: That sounds like a shortcut. It's the opposite. A mortarless wall lets groundwater seep through the stone into the tank from every side,

scenes.image_prompt: section of the whole tank filling from the surrounding earth, not just the base
scenes.animation_prompt: water ingress glows around the full perimeter, level rising evenly
scenes.narration_segment: so the well fills from the whole body of earth around it, not just the bottom.

scenes.image_prompt: the mortarless wall mid-tremor, blocks shifted a hair but holding, no cracks
scenes.narration_segment: And a wall with no rigid mortar can shift a little without shattering — one reason stepwells have ridden out earthquakes that flattened prouder buildings.

scenes.image_prompt: a spare diagrammatic emblem — no pump, no gear, a single downward gravity arrow over the well

texts.text: No pumps. No power. Just gravity.

scenes.narration_segment: No pumps. No moving parts. No power source but gravity and the seasons.

scenes.image_prompt: wide of the whole machine at rest, one quiet instruction fulfilled
scenes.narration_segment: The machine has exactly one instruction, and it has never once stopped obeying it.

scenes.image_prompt: standing-at-the-rim view of the immense step lattice, the full scale landing
scenes.animation_prompt: slow parallax as the eye falls into the lattice
scenes.narration_segment: Stand at the rim and the scale finally lands. Three thousand five hundred steps.

scenes.image_prompt: sweeping close of the hypnotic diamond step geometry, the most-photographed angle
scenes.narration_segment: Thirteen storeys of them, folded into that hypnotic diamond geometry that's made Chand Baori one of the most photographed staircases on Earth —

scenes.image_prompt: tight on the mathematically exact lattice, near-fractal repetition of steps
scenes.narration_segment: a pattern so exact that one historian simply called it 'a mathematical marvel.'

scenes.image_prompt: the north face — a tall multi-storey pillared gallery and royal pavilion rising opposite the stepped walls
scenes.narration_segment: The fourth side — the north — breaks the rhythm: a tall pillared gallery rising several storeys, with a pavilion where the local rulers could sit above the water.

scenes.image_prompt: a tiny silhouette descending into the shaded lower levels where the light cools
scenes.animation_prompt: slow descent; light temperature shifts from warm to cool shade
scenes.narration_segment: Because this was never only waterworks. Descend those steps on a blistering afternoon and something else happens — the air cools.

scenes.image_prompt: diagram comparing hot surface air and cool base, a small thermometer motif at the bottom of the shaft
scenes.narration_segment: At the bottom it holds a temperature five to six degrees cooler than the surface.

scenes.image_prompt: the shaded tank level as a communal refuge, tiny silhouettes gathered by the water
scenes.narration_segment: So the well was also a refuge: a shaded room at the bottom of the heat, where the village gathered,

scenes.image_prompt: the Harshat Mata temple beside the well, a carved sandstone shrine
scenes.narration_segment: and where — beside a well named for a king — a temple to Harshat Mata, the goddess of joy, still stands.

scenes.image_prompt: tiny silhouettes of women descending the steps at dawn with round water pots
scenes.animation_prompt: figures move gently down the steps; long soft shadows lengthen
scenes.narration_segment: And for most of its life, the people who kept this place alive were women, walking these steps at dawn and dusk with pots on their hips,

scenes.image_prompt: travellers and pilgrims resting in the cool gallery, a calm wide
scenes.narration_segment: and travellers resting in the cool before the next stretch of road.

scenes.image_prompt: a framed 'famous photograph' vignette of the lattice, inviting a second look
scenes.narration_segment: Here's what I think most people walk straight past when they see those famous photographs.

scenes.image_prompt: a reveal that reframes the staircase itself as the machine — the steps read as the working mechanism
scenes.animation_prompt: a highlight ripples across every step so the whole stair reads as one mechanism
scenes.narration_segment: We look at Chand Baori and see a staircase — a beautiful, punishing way down to the water. But the staircase isn't the route to the machine. The staircase is the machine.

scenes.image_prompt: side-by-side section — a fixed-depth well versus the range-spanning stepwell against a swinging water column
scenes.animation_prompt: the water column swings up and down; the fixed well fails while the stepwell keeps tracking it
scenes.narration_segment: A vertical well solves a fixed-depth problem. Chand Baori solves a moving-boundary problem — a water surface that swings up and down through the year — and it solves it with nothing but geometry.

scenes.image_prompt: section with many faint waterline candidates marked across the entire descent, each a potential surface
scenes.animation_prompt: the waterline candidates light up one after another down the descent
scenes.narration_segment: Every one of those three and a half thousand steps is a potential waterline, so the structure automatically offers a fresh, dry edge at whatever height the water happens to be that week.

scenes.image_prompt: a quiet endurance emblem — 'zero energy, over a thousand years' as a diagrammatic motif of the well enduring the seasons
scenes.narration_segment: It's an adaptive interface, drawn in stone, running on zero energy — and it's been running for over a thousand years.

scenes.image_prompt: a merely-'pretty' framing of the steps, about to be reread as function
scenes.narration_segment: We tend to file buildings like this under 'beautiful' and move on.

scenes.image_prompt: the lattice faintly overlaid with implied geometry/arithmetic lines — beauty as a byproduct
scenes.narration_segment: But the beauty is a side effect. The lattice is doing arithmetic.

scenes.image_prompt: back to the woman at the rim, smaller now in a familiar wide composition
scenes.narration_segment: Which brings us back to the woman at the top of the steps, counting.

scenes.image_prompt: close on the woman's hands and pot at a worn step edge, generations of wear in the stone
scenes.narration_segment: She doesn't know the phrase 'hydraulic gradient,' and she doesn't need it. She has something better: a machine her ancestors cut into the ground before anyone thought to write down its date —

scenes.image_prompt: section showing successive generations meeting the water at different seasonal levels over time
scenes.accent: the meeting point at the waterline
scenes.animation_prompt: the waterline and a tiny figure shift across seasons, always meeting at a step
scenes.narration_segment: one that has met every generation of her family at the water's edge, wherever that edge happened to fall.

scenes.image_prompt: the woman at 'thirty steps' today, one active step subtly marked
scenes.animation_prompt: a quiet counting feel; light advances one step down
scenes.narration_segment: This morning it's thirty steps. Tomorrow, maybe thirty-one. And the staircase will be there for every one of them.

scenes.image_prompt: a grand wide of Chand Baori, the quiet genius of it, timeless in golden light
scenes.animation_prompt: slow majestic parallax pull-back; dust motes, warm light
scenes.narration_segment: That's the quiet genius of Chand Baori. Not that they dug deep — that they built something which never has to be told where the water went.

scenes.image_prompt: a final calm wide at golden hour, reflective sign-off mood
scenes.narration_segment: Built to last — or built to teach us why it didn't. I'll see you in the next one.

scenes.image_prompt: a receding wide of the stepwell with clean central negative space reserved for the subscribe card overlay
scenes.narration_segment: (Outro card — subscribe + next-video thumbnail, no narration.)
